Cost Pricing

Window Cleaning Cost in Salmon Arm: What You'll Actually Pay

Published 2026-05-09 · Wagon Windows

Most homeowners in Salmon Arm have no idea what window cleaning costs until they're standing on a ladder in May, squinting into the pollen-smeared sun. Transparent pricing is rare in this industry — so here's exactly what Wagon Windows charges and why, broken down by home size and service type.

Need professional window cleaning in the Shuswap?

Get an Instant Estimate (403) 837-5092

Wagon Windows Pricing for Salmon Arm Homes

Wagon Windows uses flat-rate pricing based on home size rather than a per-window count that fluctuates with every measurement. This means you get a firm number before anyone shows up at your door, and the price doesn't change unless the scope does. For residential exterior-only cleaning, pricing starts at $169 for a small home (roughly under 1,400 sq ft), $229 for a medium home (1,400–2,200 sq ft), and $299 for a large home over 2,200 sq ft. Interior and exterior combined starts at $229 for small homes. Gutter cleaning starts at $149 for single-storey and $229 for two-storey properties. Screen cleaning is priced per screen — typically $4–5 per screen — and track cleaning is available as an add-on. Hard water stain removal is quoted separately because severity varies too much to flat-rate: a minor mineral haze and a years-old calcium deposit require entirely different treatment.

  • Exterior only (small, under 1,400 sq ft): $169
  • Exterior only (medium, 1,400–2,200 sq ft): $229
  • Exterior only (large, 2,200+ sq ft): $299
  • Interior + exterior (small): from $229
  • Gutter cleaning (single-storey): $149
  • Gutter cleaning (two-storey): $229
  • Screen cleaning: $4–5 per screen
  • Hard water stain removal: custom quote

These prices reflect the Salmon Arm market and the actual time it takes to do the job properly. Low-ball quotes that surface on community boards often omit screen cleaning, skip tracks entirely, or charge extra for anything above ground level.

What Affects the Price of Window Cleaning on Your Specific Home

Three factors move a window cleaning quote more than anything else: the number and type of windows, whether you want interior service, and accessibility. Window count matters, but it's not just about quantity. Large picture windows take more time than a standard double-hung sash. Windows above the roofline that require a ladder setup at an awkward angle add time. Bay windows and specialty shapes (arched, octagonal, transom sets) require more care than a standard rectangle. Interior service adds roughly 40–60% to an exterior-only price, depending on home size. It also requires more coordination — furniture gets moved, screens come out and are cleaned separately, and the cleaner needs access to every room. If you only want interior cleaning on the main floor or in specific rooms, that can be scoped separately. Accessibility drives cost more than most people expect. A single-storey rancher in Salmon Arm's west end is straightforward. A home built into a hillside with a daylight basement — common in the Broadview or Canoe areas — may require 30-foot ladders or pole systems to reach what looks like a second-storey window from the back. Homes with extensive landscaping or decks that block ladder placement take longer to set up safely.

  • Window count and size
  • Interior vs exterior scope
  • Height and ladder access requirements
  • Add-ons: screens, tracks, hard water treatment

Seasonal Demand and Booking Lead Times

Salmon Arm's window cleaning season follows a predictable pattern, and that pattern directly affects how far in advance you need to book. Spring — specifically late April through May — is the busiest window for the entire year. Cottonwood pollen coats everything from roughly the third week of April through early June. Most homeowners want their windows cleaned after the pollen settles, which creates a hard rush period where every available cleaning slot fills within days. If you're calling in mid-May asking for a same-week appointment, you'll likely be disappointed or waiting until June. Summer brings a second wave of demand, particularly for lake-adjacent properties in Blind Bay, Sorrento, and along the Shuswap Lake shoreline. Mineral spray from the lake and summer dust accumulation push homeowners to book a mid-season clean before hosting season peaks in July and August. Fall is more flexible. September and October are typically easier to schedule, though the window closes fast once deciduous trees start dropping leaves and homeowners combine window cleaning with gutter service before freeze. Winter service is limited to above-freezing days — exterior cleaning requires temperatures above 5°C to prevent streaking and freezing runoff. Booking is easier but availability depends on weather windows. The practical advice: book spring service in March or early April. Book fall service before mid-September. Calling within a week of when you want the job done is feasible in summer and fall, but nearly impossible in peak spring.

Why Transparent Pricing Matters for Salmon Arm Homeowners

The window cleaning industry has a persistent problem with bait-and-switch pricing. A company advertises a low entry price, arrives on-site, and then adds charges for screens, for the second storey, for 'excessive buildup', or for any number of other items that should have been in the original quote. By the time the work is done, the bill is double what was quoted. Transparent pricing matters for a few reasons specific to Salmon Arm. First, many homeowners in the Shuswap have lake-facing windows with genuine hard water mineral buildup — a problem that absolutely should be disclosed and quoted separately, but it shouldn't be used as a surprise line item on invoices for homes that don't have that issue. Second, a significant portion of Salmon Arm's housing stock is older or has irregular window configurations (common in properties that were built or expanded incrementally over decades), which means 'standard' assumptions about window count often don't apply. Wagon Windows provides quotes before showing up so you know exactly what you're paying. The Calendly booking system lets you describe your home upfront, and any ambiguity gets resolved over the phone before a booking is confirmed — not when the invoice is in your hand.

Getting the Most Accurate Quote for Your Salmon Arm Home

To get the most accurate quote without surprises, a few pieces of information help: approximate square footage or the number of bedrooms (a useful proxy), whether you want interior service, whether you have screens and want them cleaned, and whether you know of any hard water staining on the glass. For homes in areas with known mineral issues — particularly those within a few hundred metres of the lake or those on well water with high iron content — mentioning this upfront allows for an accurate scope. Hard water stain removal is a separate service from a standard clean, and quoting it as part of a bundle avoids the awkward mid-job discovery conversation. If your home is on a steep lot or has unusual window placement (skylights, floor-to-ceiling glass, clerestory windows), photograph or describe it when booking. Homes in Salmon Arm's hillside neighbourhoods often look like single-storey from the street and reveal a full daylight basement from the back — a detail that affects both labour time and equipment needs. Call (403) 837-5092 or use the online booking form at wagonwindow.com. Most quotes can be confirmed same-day for standard residential jobs.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does window cleaning cost for an average Salmon Arm home?

An average Salmon Arm home in the 1,400–2,200 sq ft range runs $229 for exterior-only or from $299 for interior and exterior combined. Homes outside that range are priced up or down accordingly. Screens and gutter cleaning are separate add-ons.

Does the price change if my home has hard water stains?

Yes. Standard window cleaning removes dirt and grime but won't eliminate mineral deposits bonded to the glass. Hard water stain removal is a separate service quoted based on severity and number of affected windows. It costs more, but it's far cheaper than replacing glass.

Is interior window cleaning included in the base price?

No. The base price covers exterior only. Interior cleaning is available as an add-on or as a combined interior-exterior service, which starts at $229 for small homes. Most customers doing a full seasonal clean choose the combined option.

How far in advance do I need to book for spring cleaning?

Spring (late April through May) fills fastest. Booking in March or early April is the best way to secure your preferred date. If you're calling in May, expect availability to be limited to late May or June.
